name: Red Torpedo
description: An Italian heirloom onion with a distinctive elongated torpedo shape and deep red-purple skin. Mild and sweet with a complex flavor that intensifies beautifully when caramelized.
history: A traditional onion from Tropea, Calabria in southern Italy — the cipolla rossa di Tropea — which has been grown there since ancient times and holds PGI status in Italy. American growers cultivate it as Red Torpedo.

good.reason: Traditional companion said to improve flavor

seasonal_quirks: A short-day onion — bulbs in response to 12-hour days, making it best for southern regions or fall planting. In northern climates, start indoors very early or treat as a specialty spring planting.
harvest_cues: Tops fall over when mature. Elongated torpedo shape should be well developed — 4–6 inches long. Cure for 2–3 weeks.
variety_notes: One of the most prized onions in Italian cuisine. The red color deepens when pickled — makes beautiful pink pickled onions. Raw slices make a striking garnish.
preservation: Pickles beautifully — becomes vivid pink in vinegar. Stores 3–4 months when cured.
